title: Should --output-split and parallallel make be the default?
body:
Currently the default make rule for verilated models compiles the C++ via the *__ALL*.cpp files which include all code into a single invokation of the compiler (well, one invokation for fast path code with optimization and one invocation for slow path code with less optimizaiton). This makes the compilation of the fast path code the long pole when building the verilated mode. There is great build-time improvement to be gain from using parallel build (`make -j`). For that to work reasonably you need the `--output-split` Verilator option, which in recent versions also sets the requied `VM_PARALLEL_BUILDS=1` when invoking Make to build the mode (#2185).

Should we turn  `--outptu-split` on by default, so `make -j` does what is expected out of the box? 

Note this question also came up in #2245 

If we do turn `--output-split` on by default, the question is with wath value? I personally find it difficult to figure out the value I should provide to it, so usually end up going with some semi wild guess after a couple iterations to check basic build time and run-time speed when I'm setting up a project, but rarely have the bandwidth to revisit it afterwards.
